reasoning: The qwen model correctly identified a critical supply-chain risk in c-vendor/vendor.sh where 'git pull' after checking out a pinned commit could introduce untrusted code. This violates build reproducibility and enables potential code injection if the remote is compromised.
disagreement_analysis: Deepseek missed the security implication of 'git pull' in vendor.sh while qwen recognized it as a supply-chain risk. This script executes during build-time dependency fetching.
coverage_issues: Both models missed analyzing other build-time hooks/scripts that might execute during makepkg
false_negatives: Deepseek falsely cleared vendor.sh's 'pull' operation as safe fallback
Both models missed potential audit of other git operations in build scripts
re_audit_focus: all git operations in build scripts
learnings: Prioritize analysis of version-control operations in build scripts
Improve model training on secure git practices (avoid pulls after pinned checkouts)
Enhance file selection to ensure 100% coverage of vendor/dependency scripts
Add explicit checks for network operations in PKGBUILD functions/vendor scripts
